question: What type of instrument can be used to accurately measure an RF field?

choices.A: A receiver with an S meter
choices.B: A calibrated field-strength meter with a calibrated antenna
choices.C: A betascope with a dummy antenna calibrated at 50Ω
choices.D: An oscilloscope with a high-stability cystal marker generator

question: What is the purpose of a transmitter power supply interlock?

choices.A: to prevent unauthorized access to a transmitter
choices.B: to guarantee that you cannot accidentally transmit out of band
choices.C: to ensure that dangerous voltages are removed if the cabinet is opened
choices.D: to shut of the transmitter if too much current is drawn

question: What is normally meant by operating a transceiver in "split" mode?

choices.A: The radio is operating at half power
choices.B: The transceiver is operating from an external power source
choices.C: The transceiver is set to different transmit and receive frequencies
choices.D: The transmitter is emitting a SSB signal, as opposed to DSB operation

question: Which of these connector types is commonly used for audio signals in Amateur Radio Stations?

choices.B: BNC
choices.C: RCA Phono
choices.D: Type N

question: Which HF antenna would be the best to use fo minimizing interference?

choices.A: A quarter-wave vertical antenna
choices.B: An isotropic antenna
choices.C: A unidirectional antenna
choices.D: An Omnidirectional antenna

question: Which of the following can be determined with a field strength meter?

choices.A: The radiation resistance of an antenna
choices.B: The radiation pattern of an antenna
choices.C: The presence and amount of phase distortion of a transmitter
choices.D: The presence and amount of amplitude distortion of a transmitter

question: What are the typical characteristic impedances of coaxial cables used for antenna feedlines at amateur stations?

choices.A: 25Ω and 30Ω
choices.B: 50Ω and 75Ω
choices.C: 80Ω and 100Ω
choices.D: 500Ω and 750Ω

question: What brief statement is often used in place of "CQ" to indicate that you are listening on a repeater?

choices.A: Say "Hello test" followed by your call sign
choices.B: Say your call sign
choices.C: Say the repeater call sign followed by your call sign
choices.D: Say the letters "QSY" followed by your call sign

question: Which of the following is common practice during net operations to get the immediate attention of the net control station when reporting an emergency?

choices.A: repeat the words SOS three times followed by the call sign of the reporting station
choices.B: press the push-to-talk button three times
choices.C: begin your transmission with "Priority" or "Emergency" followed by your call sign
choices.D: play a pre-recorded emergency alert tone followed by your call sign

question: What is the formula for converting frequency to wavelength in meters?

choices.A: Wavelength in meters equals frequency in hertz mutliplied by 300
choices.B: Wavelength in meters equals frequency in hertz divided by 300
choices.C: Wavelength in meters equals frequency in megahertz divided by 300
choices.D: Wavelength in meters equals 300 divided by frequency in megahertz

question: Which of the following describes the common meaning of the

choices.A: The distance between the repeater's transmit and receive antennas
choices.B: The time delay before the repeater timer resets
choices.C: The difference between the repeater's transmit and receive frequencies
choices.D: The maximum frequency deviation permitted on the repeater's input signal

choices.A: Acid - core solder
choices.B: Silver solder

choices.D: Aluminun solder

question: Which of these items would be useful for a hidden transmetter hunt ?

choices.A: Calibrated SWR meter
choices.B: A directional antenna
choices.C: A calibrated noise bridge
choices.D: All of these choices are correct

question: What does an antenna tuner do ?

choices.A: It matches the antenna system impedance to the transciever's output empedance
choices.B: It helps a receiver automatically tune in weak stations
choices.C: It allows an antenna to be used on both transmit and receive
choices.D: It automatically selects the proper antenna for the frequency band being used

question: Which of the following is a common use for the dual VFO feature on a transceiver?

choices.A: To allow transmitting on two frequencies at once
choices.B: To permit full duplex operation, that is transmitting and receiving at the same time
choices.C: To permit ease of monitoring the transmit and receive frequencies when they are not the same
choices.D: To facilitate computer interface

choices.A: Better for measuring computer circuits
choices.B: Better for RF measurements
choices.C: Better precision for most uses
choices.D: Faster response

question: What could be a symptom of a ground loop somewhere in your station?

choices.A: You receive reports of "hum" on your station's transmitted signal
choices.B: The SWR reading for one or more antennas is suddenly very high
choices.C: An item of station equipment starts to draw excessive amounts of current
choices.D: you recieve reports of harmonic interference from your station

choices.A: Conductance
choices.B: Impedance
choices.C: Receive signal strenth

question: Which of the following phone emission uses the narrowest frequency bandwidth?

choices.A: Single sideband
choices.B: Double sideband

choices.D: Frequency modulation

choices.A: 42 feet

choices.B: To measure the frequency of an Audio Oscillator
choices.C: Measure the reference frequency in a PLL circuit

question: What is intermodulation interference?

choices.A: Interference caused by odd order harmonics of a transmitter fundamental frequency
choices.B: Interferrence caused by the combining several signals in a non-linear device producing new unwanted frequencies
choices.C: Interference caused by a shift in the carrier frequency of an AM transmitter
choices.D: Interference caused by a signals arriving from different paths

question: What is reciever densensitization?

choices.A: When the reciever front-end is misaligned
choices.B: defective RF amplifier in the recciever
choices.C: The reciever is not tuned to the correct frequency
choices.D: Overloading of a reciever front--end by a strong off-channel signal causing a reduction in sensitivity

question: What is cross-modulation?

choices.A: The transfer of the amplitude modulation of a strong unwanted carrier onto the wanted carrierm caused by non-linearity in the RF amplifier or mixer of a reciever
choices.B: Modulation produced after passing through a zero-crossing detetor
choices.C: Modulation based on the zero-crossing of an RF signal
choices.D: Modulation which is a cross between anAM and FM mode

question: What type of electrical circuit is created when a fuse blows?

choices.A: A closed circuit
choices.B: A bypass circuit
choices.C: An open circuit
choices.D: A short circuit

choices.A: Superposition

choices.D: Phase-inversion

choices.A: Your signal is strong enough to overcome all reciever
